meta.title: OKDP - Open Kubernetes Data Platform | 100% Open Source
meta.description: OKDP is a free, open-source, and cloud-native data platform designed for Kubernetes. Built by the community, for the community.
meta.keywords: Open Kubernetes Data Platform, OKDP, Apache Iceberg, Polaris, Trino, Spark, Airflow, Superset, MLOps, Kubernetes, Data Platform

hero.title: Open Kubernetes Data Platform
hero.subtitle: A free, open-source and cloud-native data platform built on Kubernetes. Modular, sovereign, and community-driven.

about.title: What is OKDP?
about.description1: OKDP (Open Kubernetes Data Platform) is a comprehensive data management services platform composed of containerized open source software and products, running on Kubernetes infrastructure.
about.description2: OKDP addresses the complete data lifecycle: collection, storage, processing, analysis, and data exposition. It's modular by design—deploy all components or select only what you need.

cards.title: Data Centric
cards.description: Comprehensive platform covering the entire data lifecycle with built-in governance, facilitating data sharing and minimizing duplication.

cards.title: Cloud Native
cards.description: Kubernetes-native platform designed for modern cloud environments with high availability, scalability, and multi-cloud support.

cards.title: True Open Source
cards.description: 100% open source with Apache V2 license. Complete control of the technology lifecycle from build to deployment, with no vendor lock-in.

cards.title: Sovereignty & Cost Control
cards.description: Maintain full autonomy over your data infrastructure while eliminating licensing costs. Free to use, modify, and deploy at any scale.

cards.title: Modular & Future-Proof
cards.description: Flexible architecture adapts to your needs. Built on Kubernetes to prevent technical debt, ensuring sustainability and continuous modernization.

cards.title: Community-Driven
cards.description: Built by the community, for the community. Open collaboration from public and private organizations with a growing ecosystem.

architecture.title: Modular Architecture
architecture.subtitle: OKDP's architecture is built around two major layers: an ecosystem of reference Data & AI Modules and a unified Control Plane.
architecture.modulesSubtitle: A catalog of reference open source tools. Usable individually or combined, without depending on the OKDP Control Plane.

modules.title: Ingestion & Streaming
modules.desc: Connectivity, ETL ingestion and real-time processing.

modules.title: Lakehouse & Analytics
modules.desc: High-performance SQL engines and modern lakehouse.

modules.title: Data Science
modules.desc: Interactive environments for exploration and analysis.

modules.title: AI & MLOps
modules.desc: MLOps platform and language model inference.

architecture.controlPlaneSubtitle: OKDP's automation layer: orchestration, multi-tenant isolation, and governance for a turnkey experience across the entire stack.

controlPlane.title: Server / UI / CLI
controlPlane.desc: Unified web portal and interfaces for administrators and users.

controlPlane.title: Project & Quota Management
controlPlane.desc: Secure multi-tenant isolation and per-project resource limit management.

controlPlane.title: Auth & Secrets Management
controlPlane.desc: End-to-end OIDC authentication and secure secrets and RBAC management.

controlPlane.title: Observability
controlPlane.desc: Centralized collection of metrics, logs and traces across the entire platform.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.teaser: First release v1.0.0 planned for June 2026.

community.title: Join Our Community
community.subtitle: OKDP is built by the community, for the community. Join us in shaping the future of open-source data platforms.

meeting.title: Weekly Technical Meeting
meeting.desc: Every Wednesday at 10:00 AM (CET) - Contact us to receive the meeting details.

contribute.title: Call for Contributions
contribute.desc: Help us build the Open Kubernetes Data Platform. Whether you enjoy infrastructure, data engineering, or docs, there is room for you.

tosit.title: About TOSIT
tosit.desc1: OKDP is a project initiated by DGFiP, joined by Orange and other organizations, within the TOSIT association (The Open Source I Trust). The goal is to provide a sovereign, powerful, and fully open-source data & AI technology stack accessible to all.
tosit.desc2: The association brings together numerous companies and administrations, including BPCE (Banque Populaire, Caisse d'Epargne et Natixis), Société Générale, among others. It also hosts the TDP project, initiated by DGFiP and EDF.
tosit.desc3: Participation in TOSIT projects is open to all.

footer.copyright: © 2026 TOSIT — The Open Source I Trust.
footer.license: Crafted by the community • Licensed under Apache License v2.0

meta.title: Roadmap — OKDP | Open Kubernetes Data Platform
meta.description: Track the content, priorities and progress of the OKDP platform by version.

roadmapPage.title: OKDP Roadmap
roadmapPage.subtitle: Track the content, priorities and progress by version.

legend.title: Status legend
legend.inProgress: In progress — active development or integration
legend.todo: To do — identified, but not started for this version

v1.title: v1.0.0
v1.modulesSubtitle: A catalog of reference open source tools. Usable individually or combined, without depending on the OKDP Control Plane (UI/Server).

features.text: Dedicated Helm chart for Spark History Server
Spark Web Proxy extension (real-time job UI access)
Spark Docker images with automated build pipeline
Fine-grained authorization via OPA (Open Policy Agent)
Identity propagation from Trino to Polaris
JWT token propagation or Impersonation (Trino Auth)
OKDP-optimized Docker images for JupyterLab environments

v1.controlPlaneSubtitle: OKDP's automation layer: orchestration, multi-tenant isolation, and developer experience (DX) for a turnkey experience across the entire stack.

features.text: Local users and groups management (via Kubauth)
Isolated spaces creation via Project CRD (basic RBAC)
Project Secrets management (Vault via token only)
Application submission via Spark Operator (UI and kubectl)
Control Plane integration: Trino, Superset, Polaris and Airflow
Local sandbox (Kind + Flux) with legacy components
Demo Application: End-to-end use case (Ingestion, Spark & Airflow)
User Guide: Module & service usage via console

future.title: Future Evolutions
future.subtitle: Themes and modules identified to enrich the platform after the v1.0.0 release.

features.text: LLM Serving — Language model inference (vLLM, TGI)
Generic Service Catalog — Self-service interface for deploying custom extra services
Quota Management — Fine-grained Kubernetes resource management
Marketplace — Expose custom modules or components
Platform Health — Centralized module metrics & logs collection
Full GitOps Mode — Support for full GitOps deployment mode
